reason: The statement applies because the text describes how the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD, were delivered into the hand of the Midianites, and cried out to the LORD for help. The LORD then sent a prophet and an angel to deliver them through Gideon, a man chosen by God to save Israel from the Midianites. This narrative aligns with the theme of a righteous man (Gideon) being protected by Yahweh while dealing with the wicked (Midianites).
